items.briefing:
The Australian Cyber Security Centre (ACSC) has issued a warning about exposed login credentials linked to Fortinet firewalls and VPN gateways — devices commonly used by Australian organisations to secure remote access. If your organisation uses Fortinet products, IT teams should urgently check for compromised accounts and reset credentials. Separately, a Cloudflare report highlights that Australian civil society organisations — including charities, advocacy groups, and NGOs — are increasingly being targeted by cyberattacks, suggesting smaller or mission-driven organisations should not assume they are low-priority targets.

**Practical tip:** If your organisation uses Fortinet firewalls or VPN products, ask your IT provider today whether your credentials have been checked and changed in response to the ACSC warning.

featured.title: ACSC warns of reported credential exposure affecting Fortinet firewalls and VPN gateways
featured.quote: ACSC alert on widespread Fortinet credential exposure poses immediate risk to Australian infrastructure.
